Container.Name: ai-tg-bot
Container.Overview: Single-container Pi-powered Telegram agent with SQLite, persistent sessions, retrieval, files, images, web tools, and lazy embedded per-user BoxLite VMs.
Container.Requires: Telegram, OpenRouter, and Tavily API keys; /dev/kvm; cgroup v2 mounted with nsdelegate; and Docker support for private writable cgroups. Codex OAuth and an external Docling server are optional. The template runs one non-privileged container.

Config.Name: Appdata
Config.Description: Persistent SQLite database, Pi sessions/OAuth credentials, and the legacy bash tree retained for migration and rollback.

Config.Name: BoxLite Shared Data
Config.Description: Persistent user workspaces and canonical managed chat files. The entrypoint aligns this tree with APP_UID:APP_GID; keep the default 1000:1000 when using the published agent image.

Config.Name: BoxLite Runtime State
Config.Description: Dedicated embedded BoxLite runtime home. Do not share this path with another bot replica, live check, or BoxLite daemon.

Config.Name: Telegram Bot Token
Config.Description: BotFather token used to receive updates and call Telegram APIs.

Config.Name: OpenRouter API Key
Config.Description: OpenRouter key used for inference fallback, fallback image generation, and retrieval embeddings.

Config.Name: Tavily API Key
Config.Description: Tavily key used by web search and page extraction tools.

Config.Name: BoxLite Image
Config.Description: Agent VM image. Pin a sha-* tag in production.

Config.Name: BoxLite Guest User
Config.Description: Linux username, numeric UID, or UID:GID used for VM startup and every command. Named users must exist in the image; use root or 0 for images such as python:slim.

Config.Name: Application UID
Config.Description: Non-root UID used for Node and persistent data. Keep 1000 with the published agent image so guest and host workspace ownership match.

Config.Name: Application GID
Config.Description: Primary non-root GID used for Node and persistent data.

Config.Name: Docling URL
Config.Description: Optional external Docling server for DOCX, scanned PDFs, and PDFs without usable native text. Leave empty for the default one-container deployment.

Config.Name: Database URL
Config.Description: SQLite or PostgreSQL database URL.

Config.Name: Pi Agent Directory
Config.Description: Persistent Pi configuration, OAuth credentials, and Telegram-thread JSONL sessions.

Config.Name: Legacy Bash Root
Config.Description: Pre-BoxLite workspace and managed-file source retained during migration and rollback.

Config.Name: Agent Shared Root
Config.Description: Bot-side path to the same shared filesystem used for BoxLite workspaces.

Config.Name: Managed File Root
Config.Description: Canonical chat-file store, kept outside per-user VM mounts.

Config.Name: Codex Main Model
Config.Description: Pi openai-codex model for main turns when Codex OAuth is configured.

Config.Name: Codex Helper Model
Config.Description: Pi openai-codex model for isolated helper work.

Config.Name: OpenRouter Main Model
Config.Description: OpenRouter main model used without Codex or after retryable Codex failures.

Config.Name: OpenRouter Helper Model
Config.Description: OpenRouter helper model used without Codex or after retryable Codex failures.

Config.Name: OpenRouter Image Model
Config.Description: OpenRouter model used for image generation when Codex is unavailable.

Config.Name: OpenRouter Embedding Model
Config.Description: OpenRouter vector model for chunked large-document retrieval.

Config.Name: Model Context Tokens
Config.Description: Context window advertised for both internal Pi routing models.

Config.Name: Pi Thinking Level
Config.Description: Pi thinking level: off, minimal, low, medium, high, xhigh, or max.

Config.Name: Pi Turn Timeout
Config.Description: Maximum milliseconds for a Pi turn. Set to 0 to disable the hard cap.

Config.Name: Thread Title Timeout
Config.Description: Maximum milliseconds for an isolated helper-model topic-title call. Set to 0 to disable the hard cap.

Config.Name: Image Timeout
Config.Description: Maximum milliseconds for Codex or OpenRouter image generation. Set to 0 to disable the hard cap.

Config.Name: Docling Timeout
Config.Description: Docling conversion timeout in milliseconds.

Config.Name: File Inline Tokens
Config.Description: Maximum estimated file tokens to inline before chunking and indexing.

Config.Name: Bash Timeout
Config.Description: Maximum milliseconds for one BoxLite bash execution.

Config.Name: BoxLite Idle Stop
Config.Description: Stop a user's VM after this many idle milliseconds; disk state is retained.

Config.Name: BoxLite Provision Timeout
Config.Description: Bot-side upper bound in milliseconds for image acquisition and VM startup. BoxLite may report its own earlier native timeout.

Config.Name: Bash Max Output
Config.Description: Maximum characters returned for each bash stdout or stderr stream.

Config.Name: Draft Update Interval
Config.Description: Minimum milliseconds between Telegram draft/status edits.

Config.Name: Onboarding Timezone Delay
Config.Description: Delay before the onboarding timezone prompt.

Config.Name: Log Level
Config.Description: Minimum logger level: debug, info, warn, or error.
